author: Akien (the distinction) + CC (the test and the consequences)
supersedes: maintenance-is-an-axis-not-a-class — which had the shape half-right and the cause wrong
status: converging, NOT settled. Akien said 'maybe'. Not cast; no gates bound.
the_distinction:
AKIEN, 2026-07-22: 'maybe the distinctions are TICKETS (their intentions become part of the fabric of the system immediately) and TASKS (things that need to be done by the system, but do not define the system). Both are workflows.'

Both are workflow instances — mutable strings, same machinery. What differs is whether the intention joins the FABRIC: the set of records that say what the system IS.
why_this_beats_the_earlier_read:
CC had called it an AXIS — 'what becomes of the ticket when it resolves: FREEZE into history, or DEPOT as a receipt.' That is the CONSEQUENCE, not the cause. Akien's version names the cause, and freeze-vs-depot then falls out of it rather than needing to be decided. Keep the cause; the disposition is derived.

It also explains the word IMMEDIATELY, which is load-bearing. A ticket moves the system's definition at CAST — before any work is done. That is exactly why its voyage must freeze beside the code: the fabric changed, so provenance is owed (Law 5). A task can be cast, run, and completed with the definition never moving — so there is no component whose charter its story is provenance OF. Depot is not a policy choice; it is what remains when nothing downstream needs the record.
the_test:
THE SEED TEST — does replaying this intention on a BARE MACHINE contribute to regrowing the system? Ticket if yes, task if no.

- Install the usage-widget hook: essential. The box cannot run Cairn's instrumentation without it. TICKET.
- Yank the UU debris from ~/bin: a no-op on a fresh box; there is no UU there. TASK.

This is not a new gate. It is the test `intentions-other/` and `intentions/` already apply (the seed claim: carry that one folder to a bare machine and regrow the system). Ticket-vs-task therefore costs no new physics — /sorted asks a question the stores already ask (Law 1: don't re-derive the settled).

COROLLARY, sharp: a task's intention does NOT berth in `intentions-other/`. If it did, the seed would regrow instructions for cleaning a machine that was never dirty — the folder stops being a seed and becomes a diary. Where task intentions DO berth is open; the three-roots rule points at instance-space (~/.cairn/) for receipts and at commons only for what would lose knowledge if lost.
recurrence_collapses: Akien's two cases — 'the intention might be really done' vs 'recurrent maintenance gets a new workflow instance next time its trigger rolls' — are one mechanism, not two. The standing REGIME is fabric ('host seals expire; re-verify on a horizon') and is a TICKET, cast once. Each firing is a TASK. A one-shot is the same shape with a trigger that will never fire again (UU stopped being a live source of debris). This also retires CC's earlier objection about nothing-is-terminal: no intention closes; the disposable thing was always the instance.
tasks_can_graduate: The yank is a task, but the rule it justifies — 'no dead-predecessor debris on PATH' — is fabric. A task that discovers a rule SPAWNS a ticket. Without this the boundary is a trap door and knowledge found during maintenance is thrown away with the receipt.
the_danger: If TASK carries fewer gates than TICKET, gravity pulls everything toward task and the fabric quietly stops recording itself — the UU disease in a new costume. So the DEFAULT must be TICKET, and you argue your way OUT of the fabric by passing the seed test. Never into it by asserting 'this is just maintenance.' Whoever builds the /sorted gate should make ticket the fall-through and task the branch that must justify itself.
confirms_the_earlier_call: Maintenance is still not a fifth node_class. Ticket-vs-task cuts ACROSS the classes — it splits host-seam work down the middle (install the hook = ticket; yank the debris = task), which a class by definition cannot do. What Akien found sits on the workflow, not on the node.
term_check: MEASURED 2026-07-22 before coining: `task` is free. The only occurrence of the word anywhere in cairn/ or CairnCommons/ is `resolver-calls-per-solved-task` (MAP.md:190, 500), a unit of work in the demo metric — a different sense, no collision with a workflow kind.
open: Where does a task's intention berth, concretely? (Not intentions-other. ~/.cairn/ receipts + commons only when losing it loses knowledge?)
Does a task get a lighter gate-set than a ticket, or the same set with a different disposition? The danger clause says: not lighter without an argument.
Does the ticket/task flag get set at /intent (cheapest gate) or at /sorted (where typing happens)? The seed test is answerable at /intent, which argues for early.
